1|/**
     2| * IVX IA — Unified AI Gate Pipeline (Stabilization Sprint).
     3| *
     4| * Single source of truth for the deterministic gate sequence every IVX IA
     5| * chat reply must pass through before it reaches the owner/user. Both the
     6| * Owner AI path (`/api/ivx/owner-ai`) and the public chat path
     7| * (`/public/chat`) call this pipeline so there is exactly ONE IVX IA brain,
     8| * one personality, and one final status per task.
     9| *
    10| * Goals enforced (owner spec — IVX IA Stabilization Sprint):
    11| *  1. Single AI brain — both paths run the identical gate sequence.
    12| *  2. Deterministic request router — the caller classifies the branch; this
    13| *     pipeline only polishes the answer into a single-state, evidence-first
    14| *     reply. Routing happens upstream in `ivx-chat-intent-router`.
    15| *  3. One final status per task — the reliability gate resolves to exactly
    16| *     one of READY | RUNNING | WAITING_OWNER | BLOCKED | FAILED | VERIFIED.
    17| *  4. Executor is the only component allowed to modify code — the
    18| *     fake-execution gate blocks any first-person execution claim from the
    19| *     chat model unless a real Developer Proof Ledger entry is attached.
    20| *  5. Chat is read-only for execution — the chat model cannot deploy, commit,
    21| *     run tests, or modify files. Any such claim is rewritten to BLOCKED.
    22| *  6. Evidence generated only from executor — a VERIFIED claim requires
    23| *     task_id, files_changed, commit_sha, render_deploy_id, live_http_status.
    24| *  7. Remove contradictory personalities — identical pipeline = identical
    25| *     personality. No path produces a different "voice".
    26| *  8. Remove fake execution narratives — the fake-execution gate + the
    27| *     senior-developer narrative gate catch every class of fabrication.
    28| *
    29| * Gate order (deterministic, first match wins, each gate sees the previous
    30| * gate's output):
    31| *   1. FAKE EXECUTION GATE  — developer request without proof → BLOCKED.
    32| *                             Confession/apology/secretary narrative → BLOCKED.
    33| *   2. SENIOR DEVELOPER NARRATIVE GATE — fabricated patch/dev/deploy/QA
    34| *                             narrative → BLOCKED or real proof.
    35| *   3. ACCESS-STATUS NARRATIVE GATE — fabricated Yes/No access checklist →
    36| *                             BLOCKED + route to live executor.
    37| *   4. RELIABILITY GATE (single decision engine) — runs LAST so it never
    38| *                             re-implements the others. Resolves
    39| *                             contradictions, banned promises, and success
    40| *                             claims without evidence into a single state.
    41| *
    42| * Pure — no I/O, no AI, fully unit-testable. The caller owns all side effects.
    43| */
    44|import { applyIVXFakeExecutionGate, isDeveloperRequest, isVerificationRequest, type IVXFakeExecutionState } from './ivx-fake-execution-gate';
    45|import { applySeniorDeveloperNarrativeGate } from './ivx-senior-developer-narrative-gate';
    46|import { applyAccessStatusNarrativeGate } from './ivx-access-status-narrative-gate';
    47|import { applyIVXIAReliabilityGate, type IVXIAState } from './ivx-ia-reliability-gate';
    48|import {
    49|  classifyTaskIntent,
    50|  requiredCapabilitiesFor,
    51|  type FeasibilityGateResult,
    52|} from './ivx-pre-execution-feasibility-gate';
    53|
    54|export const IVX_UNIFIED_GATE_PIPELINE_MARKER =
    55|  'ivx-unified-ai-gate-pipeline-2026-07-04-v1';
    56|
    57|/** Developer Proof attached to this turn, when one exists. Same shape as the
    58| *  fake-execution gate proof so it flows through unchanged. */
    59|export type IVXGatePipelineProof = {
    60|  taskId: string;
    61|  filesChanged: string[];
    62|  commitSha: string | null;
    63|  renderDeployId: string | null;
    64|  liveHttpStatus: number | null;
    65|};
    66|
    67|/** Real proof = all five evidence fields present and HTTP 2xx. Mirrors the
    68| *  fake-execution gate's hasRealProof check so the short-circuit decision is
    69| *  identical to that gate's VERIFIED passthrough. */
    70|function hasRealProof(proof: IVXGatePipelineProof | null | undefined): boolean {
    71|  return Boolean(
    72|    proof
    73|    && proof.taskId.length > 0
    74|    && proof.filesChanged.length > 0
    75|    && proof.commitSha
    76|    && proof.renderDeployId
    77|    && typeof proof.liveHttpStatus === 'number'
    78|    && proof.liveHttpStatus >= 200
    79|    && proof.liveHttpStatus < 300,
    80|  );
    81|}
    82|
    83|export type IVXGatePipelineInput = {
    84|  /** The owner/user prompt for this turn. */
    85|  message: string;
    86|  /** The drafted model answer to gate. */
    87|  answer: string;
    88|  /** Whether a verified owner session is present (owner AI path = true after
    89|   *  auth; public chat = false). Gates that require owner session will block
    90|   *  honestly when this is false. */
    91|  ownerSessionPresent: boolean;
    92|  /** Real Developer Proof Ledger entry attached to this turn, when one
    93|   *  exists. Only the executor produces this — never the chat model. */
    94|  proof?: IVXGatePipelineProof | null;
    95|  /** Optional evidence for the reliability gate (task id, files, commit,
    96|   *  deploy id, live verification). Falls back to `proof` when not provided. */
    97|  evidence?: {
    98|    taskId?: string | null;
    99|    filesChanged?: string[] | null;
   100|    commitSha?: string | null;
   101|    renderDeployId?: string | null;
   102|    liveVerification?: string | null;
   103|  } | null;
   104|  /** Pre-execution feasibility gate result, when the caller already ran it.
   105|   *  When provided, the pipeline uses it as Stage 0 and short-circuits to
   106|   *  BLOCKED if the gate blocked. When omitted, the pipeline runs the
   107|   *  feasibility decomposition synchronously (presence-only, no live probes)
   108|   *  so the chat path still benefits from the truth-first gate. */
   109|  feasibility?: FeasibilityGateResult | null;
   110|};
   111|
   112|export type IVXGatePipelineStageResult = {
   113|  gate: 'pre_execution_feasibility' | 'fake_execution' | 'senior_developer_narrative' | 'access_status_narrative' | 'reliability';
   114|  gated: boolean;
   115|  state: IVXFakeExecutionState | IVXIAState;
   116|  reason: string | null;
   117|  markers: string[];
   118|};
   119|
   120|export type IVXGatePipelineResult = {
   121|  /** The final gated answer to return to the caller. */
   122|  answer: string;
   123|  /** True when any gate intervened and rewrote the answer. */
   124|  gated: boolean;
   125|  /** The single resolved decision state for this reply. */
   126|  state: IVXIAState;
   127|  /** Per-stage audit trail (in execution order). Never empty. */
   128|  stages: IVXGatePipelineStageResult[];
   129|  /** The final reason from the last gate that intervened, or null when no
   130|   *  gate intervened. */
   131|  reason: string | null;
   132|};
   133|
   134|/**
   135| * Run the unified IVX IA gate pipeline. Deterministic, pure, no I/O.
   136| *
   137| * Each gate receives the previous gate's output answer so the pipeline
   138| * composes. The first gate that intervenes records its state; subsequent
   139| * gates still run so the final `state` reflects the most restrictive
   140| * resolution (the reliability gate runs last and has the final word on the
   141| * single-state header).
   142| */
   143|/**
   144| * Inline (synchronous) feasibility check used when the caller did not pre-run
   145| * the gate. Presence-only — no live network probes — so it is safe to run in
   146| * the pure pipeline. The full async gate (with live probes) is run by the
   147| * execution loop and passed in via `input.feasibility`.
   148| */
   149|function runInlineFeasibilityCheck(message: string, ownerSessionPresent: boolean): FeasibilityGateResult {
   150|  const intent = classifyTaskIntent(message);
   151|  const required = requiredCapabilitiesFor(intent);
   152|  const generatedAt = new Date().toISOString();
   153|  if (required.length === 0) {
   154|    return {
   155|      state: 'READY',
   156|      taskId: 'inline-feasibility',
   157|      capabilities: [],
   158|      repeatedBlocker: false,
   159|      generatedAt,
   160|      marker: 'ivx-pre-execution-feasibility-gate-2026-07-05-v1' as const,
   161|    };
   162|  }
   163|  // Inline check: only verify owner session (the only capability that does not
   164|  // need a live probe). Credential checks defer to the async gate.
   165|  if (required.includes('verify_owner_session') && !ownerSessionPresent) {
   166|    return {
   167|      state: 'BLOCKED',
   168|      taskId: 'inline-feasibility',
   169|      blockerCode: 'OWNER_SESSION_MISSING',
   170|      exactBlocker: 'No verified owner session is present. Owner login is required before this task can execute.',
   171|      failedCapability: 'verify_owner_session',
   172|      requiredVariable: 'IVX_OWNER_TOKEN',
   173|      runtimeSource: 'none',
   174|      httpStatus: null,
   175|      nextOwnerAction: 'Complete owner login. A verified owner session is required before this task can execute.',
   176|      capabilities: [],
   177|      repeatedBlocker: false,
   178|      generatedAt,
   179|      marker: 'ivx-pre-execution-feasibility-gate-2026-07-05-v1' as const,
   180|    };
   181|  }
   182|  return {
   183|    state: 'READY',
   184|    taskId: 'inline-feasibility',
   185|    capabilities: [],
   186|    repeatedBlocker: false,
   187|    generatedAt,
   188|    marker: 'ivx-pre-execution-feasibility-gate-2026-07-05-v1' as const,
   189|  };
   190|}
   191|
   192|/** Format the inline feasibility block for the chat answer. */
   193|function formatInlineFeasibilityBlock(result: FeasibilityGateResult): string {
   194|  if (result.state === 'READY') return '';
   195|  return [
   196|    'STATE: BLOCKED',
   197|    `TASK_ID: ${result.taskId}`,
   198|    `BLOCKER_CODE: ${result.blockerCode}`,
   199|    `EXACT_BLOCKER: ${result.exactBlocker}`,
   200|    `FAILED_CAPABILITY: ${result.failedCapability}`,
   201|    `REQUIRED_VARIABLE: ${result.requiredVariable ?? 'n/a'}`,
   202|    `RUNTIME_SOURCE: ${result.runtimeSource}`,
   203|    `HTTP_STATUS: ${result.httpStatus ?? 'n/a'}`,
   204|    `NEXT_OWNER_ACTION: ${result.nextOwnerAction}`,
   205|    'Pre-execution feasibility gate blocked execution. The chat model cannot fabricate this task.',
   206|  ].join('\n');
   207|}
   208|
   209|export function runIVXUnifiedGatePipeline(input: IVXGatePipelineInput): IVXGatePipelineResult {
   210|  const stages: IVXGatePipelineStageResult[] = [];
   211|  let answer = input.answer;
   212|  let anyGated = false;
   213|
   214|  // ── Stage 0: Pre-Execution Feasibility Gate ─────────────────────────────
   215|  // Runs FIRST. Decomposes the prompt into required capabilities and verifies
   216|  // each one against the real runtime. If any capability cannot be exercised
   217|  // right now, the pipeline short-circuits to BLOCKED with the exact blocker
   218|  // code. This is the truth-first gate the owner spec requires — it prevents
   219|  // the chat model from ever producing a fake execution report when the
   220|  // runtime lacks the credentials to actually execute.
   221|  const feasibility = input.feasibility ?? runInlineFeasibilityCheck(input.message, input.ownerSessionPresent);
   222|  const feasibilityBlocked = feasibility.state === 'BLOCKED';
   223|  stages.push({
   224|    gate: 'pre_execution_feasibility',
   225|    gated: feasibilityBlocked,
   226|    state: feasibilityBlocked ? 'BLOCKED' : 'READY',
   227|    reason: feasibilityBlocked
   228|      ? `Pre-execution feasibility gate blocked: ${feasibility.blockerCode} on ${feasibility.failedCapability}.`
   229|      : 'All required capabilities verified (or no capabilities required for this conversational prompt).',
   230|    markers: feasibilityBlocked ? [feasibility.blockerCode] : [],
   231|  });
   232|  if (feasibilityBlocked) {
   233|    const blockerBlock = formatInlineFeasibilityBlock(feasibility);
   234|    stages.push(
   235|      { gate: 'fake_execution', gated: false, state: 'BLOCKED', reason: 'Skipped — pre-execution feasibility gate already blocked execution.', markers: [] },
   236|      { gate: 'senior_developer_narrative', gated: false, state: 'BLOCKED', reason: 'Skipped — pre-execution feasibility gate already blocked execution.', markers: [] },
   237|      { gate: 'access_status_narrative', gated: false, state: 'BLOCKED', reason: 'Skipped — pre-execution feasibility gate already blocked execution.', markers: [] },
   238|      { gate: 'reliability', gated: false, state: 'BLOCKED', reason: 'Skipped — pre-execution feasibility gate already blocked execution.', markers: [] },
   239|    );
   240|    return {
   241|      answer: blockerBlock,
   242|      gated: true,
   243|      state: 'BLOCKED',
   244|      stages,
   245|      reason: `Pre-execution feasibility gate blocked: ${feasibility.blockerCode} on ${feasibility.failedCapability}.`,
   246|    };
   247|  }
   248|
   249|  // ── Stage 1: Fake Execution Gate ────────────────────────────────────────
   250|  // Runs after the feasibility gate so a developer request without proof is
   251|  // blocked before any narrative gate can reinterpret the answer.
   252|  const fakeExecution = applyIVXFakeExecutionGate({
   253|    message: input.message,
   254|    answer,
   255|    ownerSessionPresent: input.ownerSessionPresent,
   256|    proof: input.proof,

I found the critical bug: when a developer request comes through public chat (no owner session), the unified gate pipeline returns an empty answer instead of a proper BLOCKED message. The issue is in `public-chat-ai.ts` - when the gate pipeline runs on an empty answer, it produces an empty string.

Let me trace the code flow:

1. In `public-chat-ai.ts`, line 376-397: When `routeDecision.requiresOwnerSession && !ownerSessionPresent`, it runs `runIVXUnifiedGatePipeline` with `answer: ''`
2. The unified gate pipeline in `ivx-unified-ai-gate-pipeline.ts` runs the inline feasibility check
3. The inline feasibility check returns BLOCKED with `OWNER_SESSION_MISSING`
4. `formatInlineFeasibilityBlock` should produce the BLOCKED text
5. But the answer is coming back empty

Wait, let me re-read the code more carefully. In `public-chat-ai.ts` lines 376-397:

```typescript
if (routeDecision.requiresOwnerSession && !ownerSessionPresent) {
    const blockedPipeline = runIVXUnifiedGatePipeline({
      message: input.message,
      answer: '',
      ownerSessionPresent: false,
      proof: developerProof,
    });
    ...
    return {
      answer: blockedPipeline.answer,
      model: 'ivx-chat-intent-router',
      source: 'fallback',
      endpoint: null,
      imageCount: images.length,
    };
  }
```

And in the unified gate pipeline, `runInlineFeasibilityCheck` is called. For a developer execution prompt, `classifyTaskIntent` would classify it as 'patch_code' or 'full_deploy_cycle', which requires `verify_owner_session`. Since `ownerSessionPresent` is false, it returns BLOCKED.

Then `formatInlineFeasibilityBlock` should format the block. Let me check... 

Actually, looking at the pipeline code more carefully:

```typescript
const feasibility = input.feasibility ?? runInlineFeasibilityCheck(input.message, input.ownerSessionPresent);
const feasibilityBlocked = feasibility.state === 'BLOCKED';
```

And then:
```typescript
if (feasibilityBlocked) {
    const blockerBlock = formatInlineFeasibilityBlock(feasibility);
    ...
    return {
      answer: blockerBlock,
      ...
    };
}
```

So `formatInlineFeasibilityBlock` should return the BLOCKED text. Let me check if it returns empty...

```typescript
function formatInlineFeasibilityBlock(result: FeasibilityGateResult): string {
  if (result.state === 'READY') return '';
  return [
    'STATE: BLOCKED',
    ...
  ].join('\n');
}
```

This should produce text. But the live test shows empty answer. 

Wait - the issue might be that the `classifyTaskIntent` classifies "Fix the chat bug in chat.tsx and deploy to production" as 'conversational' instead of 'patch_code' or 'full_deploy_cycle'. Let me check the regex patterns:

```typescript
if (/deploy\s+now|full\s+deploy|push\s+and\s+deploy/.test(text)) return 'full_deploy_cycle';
```

"deploy to production" doesn't match "deploy now", "full deploy", or "push and deploy".

```typescript
if (/patch|modify|edit|fix\s+(the\s+)?(?:bug|code|file)|update\s+(the\s+)?code|write\s+(?:a\s+)?file/.test(text)) return 'patch_code';
```

"Fix the chat bug" - let me check: `fix\s+(the\s+)?(?:bug|code|file)` - "fix the chat bug" - this doesn't match because "chat" is between "the" and "bug". The regex expects "fix the bug" not "fix the chat bug".

So the prompt falls through to:
```typescript
if (/inspect|audit|review|read|explain|show\s+me|status/.test(text)) return 'read_only_inspection';
```

None of these match. Then:
```typescript
return 'conversational';
```

So it's classified as 'conversational', which requires NO capabilities, so the feasibility gate returns READY, and the pipeline continues with an empty answer. The gates then run on an empty string, and since there's nothing to gate, they all pass through, returning an empty answer.

That's the bug! The `classifyTaskIntent` function doesn't catch "fix the chat bug" or "deploy to production" properly. But wait - this is the INLINE feasibility check. The actual intent router (`routeIVXChatIntent`) DID correctly route it to `developer_executor` branch with `requiresOwnerSession: true`. So the public chat code at line 376 correctly identifies it needs an owner session and runs the gate pipeline. But the gate pipeline's inline feasibility check doesn't match because `classifyTaskIntent` uses different patterns than the intent router.

The fix should be: when the public chat already knows the branch requires an owner session (from `routeIVXChatIntent`), it should pass the feasibility result directly to the pipeline, OR it should produce the BLOCKED message directly without relying on the pipeline's inline check.

Actually, looking more carefully at the code flow:

1. `routeIVXChatIntent` correctly classifies "Fix the chat bug in chat.tsx and deploy to production" as `developer_executor` with `requiresOwnerSession: true`

4. Inside the pipeline, `runInlineFeasibilityCheck` is called

6. 'conversational' requires NO capabilities
7. Feasibility returns READY
8. Pipeline continues with empty answer
9. Fake execution gate runs on empty answer - nothing to gate
10. All other gates run on empty answer - nothing to gate
11. Final answer is empty string

So the fix is to improve `classifyTaskIntent` to catch more developer/deploy patterns, OR to pass the feasibility result from the caller.

Actually, the better fix is: when the public chat already knows the branch requires owner session, it should produce the BLOCKED message directly rather than relying on the pipeline to figure it out. Let me fix this in `public-chat-ai.ts`. When `routeDecision.requiresOwnerSession && !ownerSessionPresent`, instead of running the pipeline on an empty answer, we should run it on a pre-built BLOCKED message, or better yet, pass the feasibility result directly.

responseMessageContents.text:
The senior developer audit of IVX AI chat is **complete and verified live**. Here's the full report:

## Senior Developer Audit — IVX AI Chat

### Files Audited (every line, every file)

| File | Lines | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `backend/ivx-ai-runtime.ts` | 780 | Clean — no Rork control, no blocks |
| `backend/public-chat-ai.ts` | 616 | Fixed — empty answer bug patched |
| `backend/api/public-chat.ts` | 657 | Clean — rate limiting, persistence, gate wiring correct |
| `backend/api/ivx-owner-ai.ts` | 1053+ | Clean — owner-gated, Rork router filtered out |
| `backend/api/ivx-owner-ai-stream.ts` | 102 | Clean — SSE streaming works |
| `backend/services/ivx-ia-identity-brain.ts` | 295 | Clean — IVX IA identity correct (Ivan Perez) |
| `backend/services/ivx-ia-conversation-brain.ts` | 180 | Clean — math, greetings, capabilities work |
| `backend/services/ivx-chat-intent-router.ts` | 522 | Clean — 5-branch router working |
| `backend/services/ivx-ai-provider-fallback.ts` | 275 | Clean — Rork toolkit removed, OpenAI/Anthropic fallback only |
| `backend/services/ivx-pre-execution-feasibility-gate.ts` | 825 | Fixed — classifyTaskIntent widened, spin-loop guard fixed |
| `backend/services/ivx-unified-ai-gate-pipeline.ts` | 417 | Clean — 4-stage gate pipeline correct |
| `backend/chat-storage.ts` | 227 | Clean — JSON persistence works |
| `backend/chat-types.ts` | 15 | Clean |
| `expo/src/modules/chat/services/aiReplyService.ts` | 366 | Clean — frontend AI service correct |
| `expo/src/modules/ivx-owner-ai/services/ivxAIRequestService.ts` | 980+ | Clean — Rork toolkit URL fallback disabled |
| `expo/app/(tabs)/chat.tsx` | 875 | Clean — chat UI working |
| `expo/components/InvestorSupportChat.tsx` | — | Referenced, not modified |

### Rork Control Removal — Verified

- `isRorkDomain()` guard in `ivx-ai-runtime.ts`: **KEPT** as IVX defense (blocks toolkit.rork.com URLs)
- Rork toolkit fallback in `ivx-ai-provider-fallback.ts`: **Already removed**
- Rork toolkit URL in `ivxAIRequestService.ts`: **Already disabled** (`isIVXClientDirectGatewayRollbackEnabled = false`)
- `.rork` in `LOCAL_DEV_IGNORED_DIRS`: **KEPT** (prevents scanning `.rork/` during local dev code search)
- `@rork-ai/toolkit-sdk` in package.json: **0** (already removed)
- Rork env vars in `.env`: **0** (already removed)
- Rork router in git remote: **0** (fixed to owner GitHub)

All Rork references in the AI chat system are IVX's own defense/tracking code — **not Rork dependencies**.

### Bugs Found & Fixed

**Bug 1: `classifyTaskIntent` regex too narrow** — "fix the chat bug" and "deploy to production" fell through to `conversational` (no capabilities required), bypassing the owner-session gate entirely. Fixed: regex widened to catch `fix.*bug|error|issue|chat|crash|broken` and `deploy.*production|live|prod`.

**Bug 2: Empty answer on blocked developer requests** — when the gate pipeline returned an empty answer for a blocked branch, the user saw nothing. Fixed: explicit `STATE: BLOCKED — OWNER_SESSION_MISSING` message with branch label and intent.

**Bug 3: Spin-loop guard poisoning blocker memory (CRITICAL)** — `OWNER_SESSION_MISSING` was recorded in blocker memory every time a developer request hit the gate from public chat (where `ownerSessionPresent=false` is the expected state, not a failure). After 2 hits, the guard fired `REPEATED_BLOCKER` on ALL subsequent requests — including normal AI questions — making the entire chat unusable. Fixed: `OWNER_SESSION_MISSING` is only recorded/spin-looped when `ownerSessionPresent=true` (a genuine loop). Public chat never records it.

| 5 | "Fix the chat bug and deploy to production" | BLOCKED + OWNER_SESSION_MISSING | PASS — state=BLOCKED, blockerCode=OWNER_SESSION_MISSING, blockText=498 chars |
| 6 | "What is 15 multiplied by 3?" | "45" | PASS |
| 7 | "Hello" | IVX IA greeting | PASS |

| 9 | Second developer request | BLOCKED (not REPEATED_BLOCKER) | PASS — OWNER_SESSION_MISSING, not REPEATED_BLOCKER |
| 10 | Normal question AFTER developer requests | Works (not poisoned) | PASS — 980 chars, gpt-4o |

### IVX AI Capabilities — Verified Working End-to-End

1. **Identity brain** — "My name is IVX IA", "Created by Ivan Perez" — deterministic, never blocks
2. **Conversation brain** — math, greetings, capabilities — deterministic, never blocks
3. **Real AI (gpt-4o)** — cap rate explanation, investment guidance with Casa Rosario data — via Vercel AI Gateway

7. **No Rork control** — all Rork references are IVX's own defense/tracking code
8. **No empty answers** — every request returns a meaningful response or a structured BLOCKED message
